title: Build spec_to_ars() for arsbridge R Package
text: one function arsbridge R package. function reads lead programmer’s completed annotated TLF shell (Word docx) ADaM specification (Excel), produces CDISC ARS v1.0 ARM-TS JSON file. JSON consumed siera::readARS() generate QC R scripts. Core principle: parser extracts converts — invent. Every variable ARS output must trace back annotation written lead programmer annotated shell. blank shells. variable inference. Style-agnostic parsing: parser must assume single annotation style. CROs different conventions — red font colour, bold/italic, brackets, parentheses, dedicated annotation column, formatting . Font colour one signal among several, primary requirement. ADaM naming convention (ADSL.AGE, ADAE.TRTEMFL='Y') structurally distinctive enough identify annotation text alone. stub cell contains "Age (years)  ADSL.AGE" unambiguously annotated regardless whether text ADSL.AGE red, black, bold, plain. parser must detect annotations using hierarchy evidence never fail extract annotation simply special formatting applied.

what: Layer 3 — ADaM Naming Pattern Detection (Text Only)
title: Build spec_to_ars() for arsbridge R Package
text: universally applicable detection layer. Many annotated shells — particularly produced -house smaller CROs — apply special formatting whatsoever. lead programmer simply appends ADaM variable name stub row label, separated whitespace, tab, delimiter. sufficient unambiguous identification ADaM naming conventions highly distinctive: Standard dataset prefix always starts AD (ADSL, ADAE, ADLB, ADTTE, etc.) Variable names 1–8 uppercase alphanumeric characters dot separator Filter conditions follow fixed grammar: DATASET.VARIABLE='VALUE' Count expressions follow fixed prose: unique USUBJID DATASET ... natural-language stub row label ("Age Informed Consent (years)", "Reason Study Discontinuation", "Subjects Least One AE") ever accidentally match patterns. text stub cell matches ADaM pattern, annotation high certainty — even text plain black indistinguishable appearance rest cell. parser must apply layer every cell regardless whether Layers 1 2 already fired. serves primary detection method (unformatted shells) confirmation method (validating coloured/formatted text actually ADaM, footnote marker decorative element). Structurally distinctive patterns — plain text unambiguous: Core ADaM regex patterns (R syntax): Splitting label annotation colour available: cell contains stub row label ADaM annotation plain text, separator typically one following (check order): Two spaces: "Age (years)  ADSL.AGE" — common convention Newline: "Age (years)\nADSL.AGE" — shells use line breaks Tab character: "Age (years)\tADSL.AGE" Square brackets: "Age (years) [ADSL.AGE]" — bracket-enclosed annotation Parentheses wrapping ADaM text: "Age (years) (ADSL.AGE)" — distinguish "(years)" checking whether parenthesised content matches ADaM pattern separator — annotation starts immediately label: Less common occurs cell contains ADaM reference display label (e.g. dedicated annotation column) cell contains ADaM reference label text: shells use dedicated annotation column cells contain nothing ADSL.AGE ADSL.SAFFL='Y'. case split_label_annotation returns label = "" annotation = "ADSL.AGE". label taken stub column row instead. False positive guard — flag annotations: Apply ADAM_ANNOTATION_PATTERN strictly — text match, annotation regardless position cell. Confidence: HIGH match full DATASET.VARIABLE reference (Patterns 1–6 ). structural uniqueness ADaM naming means plain-text match reliable colour match.
code: "Age (years)  ADSL.AGE"   Plain text, no formatting. "ADSL.AGE" matches DATASET.VARIABLE exactly.   Confidence: HIGH — no English word could match this pattern.  "Screen Failure  ADSL.SCRFFL='Y'"   The equality expression DATASET.VARIABLE='VALUE' is unique to ADaM.   Confidence: HIGH.  "Overall Survival  ADTTE.PARAMCD EQ 'OS'"   ARS comparator syntax. Unambiguous.   Confidence: HIGH.  "Concomitant Medications  unique USUBJID in ADCM where ADCM.CONTRTFL='Y'"   Count expression with dataset and filter. Unambiguous.   Confidence: HIGH.  "Discontinuation Reason  ADSL.DCSREAS not missing"   "not missing" null check pattern. Unambiguous.   Confidence: HIGH.  "Race, n (%)  ADSL.RACE"   Even embedded in a display label with comma and parentheses — ADSL.RACE   is structurally distinct from any English text.   Confidence: HIGH. ADAM_DS  <- "AD[A-Z]{1,6}" ADAM_VAR <- "[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{0,7}"  PATTERN_SIMPLE  <- paste0("\\b", ADAM_DS, "\\.", ADAM_VAR, "\\b") PATTERN_EQ      <- paste0("\\b", ADAM_DS, "\\.", ADAM_VAR, "\\s*=\\s*'[^']*'") PATTERN_ARS     <- paste0("\\b", ADAM_DS, "\\.", ADAM_VAR,                            "\\s+(?:EQ|NE|IN|NOTIN|GT|GE|LT|LE)\\s+'[^']*'") PATTERN_COUNT   <- paste0("(?i)unique\\s+USUBJID\\s+in\\s+", ADAM_DS) PATTERN_WHERE   <- paste0("\\b", ADAM_DS, "\\.", ADAM_VAR,                            "\\s+(?i:where)\\s+", ADAM_VAR, "\\s*=\\s*'[^']*'") PATTERN_NULL    <- paste0("\\b", ADAM_DS, "\\.", ADAM_VAR,                            "\\s+(?i:not\\s+(?:null|missing))")  ADAM_ANNOTATION_PATTERN <- paste(   PATTERN_SIMPLE, PATTERN_EQ, PATTERN_ARS,   PATTERN_COUNT,  PATTERN_WHERE, PATTERN_NULL,   sep = "|" ) split_label_annotation <- function(cell_text) {   # Find position of first ADaM pattern match   m <- regexpr(ADAM_ANNOTATION_PATTERN, cell_text, perl = TRUE)   if (m == -1) return(list(label = trimws(cell_text), annotation = ""))    annotation_start <- m[1]   before_match     <- substr(cell_text, 1, annotation_start - 1)    # Strip trailing separator characters to get clean label   label      <- trimws(gsub("\\s*[\\[\\(]?\\s*$", "", before_match))    # Extract full annotation (may extend beyond single match — e.g. compound OR)   annotation <- trimws(substr(cell_text, annotation_start, nchar(cell_text)))   annotation <- gsub("[\\]\\)]\\s*$", "", annotation)  # remove closing bracket if any    list(label = label, annotation = annotation) } "n"             — single letter, does not match ADAM_DS.ADAM_VAR "Mean (SD)"     — no dot-separated uppercase token "Q1, Q3"        — no ADaM dataset prefix "xxx"           — placeholder, explicitly excluded "[a]"           — footnote marker, content does not match ADaM pattern "N=125"         — sample size, not a variable reference

text: Bracket style: Detection: \\[([^\\]]+)\\] — extract bracket content, apply ADaM pattern. Separate annotation column: shells 3+ columns column 1 (last non-data column) contains annotations. Detect checking non-stub column placeholder values (xxx, xx.x) non-empty cells match ADaM pattern. Footnote-linked annotation: extracting footnotes, check ADaM patterns. footnote [] contains ADaM reference, link back rows bearing []. Flag detection_method="footnote_link".
code: "Age (years) [ADSL.AGE]"            →  label="Age (years)",  annotation="ADSL.AGE" "Screen Failure [ADSL.SCRFFL='Y']"  →  annotation="ADSL.SCRFFL='Y'" is_annotation_column <- function(col_cells) {   no_placeholders <- !any(grepl("^x+(?:\\.x+)?$", col_cells, ignore.case = TRUE))   mostly_adam     <- mean(grepl(ADAM_ANNOTATION_PATTERN,                                 col_cells[nchar(col_cells) > 0])) > 0.5   no_placeholders && mostly_adam }

code: #' Convert Annotated TLF Shell and ADaM Spec to CDISC ARS JSON #' #' @param shell_path      Path to annotated TLF shells Word document (.docx). #' @param adam_spec_path  Path to ADaM specification Excel file (.xlsx or .xls). #' @param output_path     Path for the output ARS JSON file. Default "reporting_event.json". #' @param study_id        Study identifier. Default "STUDY-001". #' @param study_name      Human-readable study name for the ReportingEvent. #' @param model           Anthropic model. Default "claude-sonnet-4-5". #' @param api_key         Anthropic API key. Reads from ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var by default. #' @param validate        If TRUE (default), cross-reference annotations against the ADaM spec. #' @param report_path     Path for the validation report Excel. Default "spec_validation_report.xlsx". #' @param verbose         Print progress. Default TRUE. #' #' @return Invisibly returns a named list: #'   \describe{ #'     \item{ars_path}{Path to the generated ARS JSON file.} #'     \item{report_path}{Path to the validation report (if validate=TRUE).} #'     \item{n_tlfs}{Number of TLF sections processed.} #'     \item{n_analyses}{Number of ARS Analysis objects created.} #'     \item{n_warnings}{Number of spec validation warnings.} #'   } #' #' @export spec_to_ars <- function(     shell_path,     adam_spec_path,     output_path  = "reporting_event.json",     study_id     = "STUDY-001",     study_name   = NULL,     model        = "claude-sonnet-4-6",     api_key      = Sys.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"),     validate     = TRUE,     report_path  = "spec_validation_report.xlsx",     verbose      = TRUE ) { ... }

title: Build spec_to_ars() for arsbridge R Package
text: Store inst/prompts/enrich_tlf_prompt.txt:
code: You are an expert CDISC ADaM and ARS programmer. You are given a parsed TLF shell section from a clinical trial. The annotations (in the "annotated_rows" field) were written by a lead programmer — they are correct and authoritative. Do NOT invent new variable names. Only use variables present in "available_variables".  TLF Section: {tlf_json}  Return a JSON object with these fields:  "analysis_type": One of CONTINUOUS, CATEGORICAL, SURVIVAL, AE_FREQUENCY, FIGURE, LISTING   - CONTINUOUS: n, mean, SD, median stats on a numeric variable   - CATEGORICAL: frequency counts n(%) of category values   - SURVIVAL: Kaplan-Meier time-to-event analysis   - AE_FREQUENCY: adverse event counts by SOC/PT hierarchy   - FIGURE: graphical output   - LISTING: subject-level data rows  "ars_method_name": Choose the closest match:   "Summary Statistics - Continuous" | "Count and Percentage" | "Subject Count" |   "Kaplan-Meier Estimate" | "AE Frequency Count" | "Listing"  "by_variable": ADaM variable used for treatment group columns. Use "TRT01A" unless   inferred otherwise from column headers.  "row_enrichments": Array with one entry per annotated_row:   {     "label":            stub_row label exactly as given,     "primary_dataset":  dataset portion of annotation (e.g. "ADSL"),     "primary_variable": variable portion (e.g. "AGE"),     "data_subset":      if annotation contains a WHERE clause beyond the population flag:                         {"dataset":"X","variable":"Y","comparator":"EQ","value":["Z"]}                         Otherwise null.     "variable_role":    "ANALYSIS" | "GROUPING" | "COUNT" | "FLAG"   }  Return ONLY valid JSON. No text outside the JSON block.

what: 4. Root causes (file:line in the arsbridge repo)
title: arsbridge — Layout Fidelity & Annotation Binding: What Happened and What To Do Next
text: Annotation reader supports one placement. .detect_annotation() (R/parse_shell_docx.R:453) reads annotation inside stub cell (colour / bold / bracket / plain DATASET.VAR). shell annotates Label -> DATASET.VAR lines table, reasonable convention arsbridge read → rows has_annot = FALSE. Footnote catch-swallows annotations. R/parse_shell_docx.R:163-167 routes post-table paragraph >10 chars footnotes test annotation runs. annotation lines flow build_ars_json ARS Footnote display section, extract_footnotes() (R/ars_to_tfrmt.R:69) ships GT source notes. Rows rebuilt ARD, shell. detect_row_roles() (R/ars_to_tfrmt.R:183) sets label_var = .ARS_ROW_LABEL (synthetic) groups ADaM variable; .tfrmt_prep_ard() (R/ars_to_tfrmt.R:371-390) labels rows variable_level (COMPLETED/DISCONTINUED). Authored stub labels order never consulted. Rows dropped / collapsed. -cell annotations, LLM produced 4 analyses 6 rows (enrolled & screen-fail dropped) pointed 3 EOSSTT, renderer dedups one block. Population level leaks columns. detect_col_var() (R/ars_to_tfrmt.R:131) picks TRT01A, carries “Screen Failure” level (52 subjects) → renders treatment column. Unifying problem: arsbridge semantics-first (variable + method → generic cross-tab). first-class model authored table layout, annotation reader supports one placement convention.

what: arsbridge: convert annotated TLF shells to CDISC ARS v1.0 JSON — arsbridge-package
title: arsbridge: convert annotated TLF shells to CDISC ARS v1.0 JSON — arsbridge-package
text: arsbridge reads lead programmer's already-annotated TLF shell Word document study's ADaM specification Excel, produces valid CDISC Analysis Results Standard (ARS) v1.0 ARM-TS JSON file consumable siera::readARS().

title: arsbridge: convert annotated TLF shells to CDISC ARS v1.0 JSON — arsbridge-package
text: Annotation reading style-agnostic uses two passes together extract many annotation variants possible: deterministic four-layer regex detector (font colour, character formatting, brackets, plain text) LLM primary reader separates display label variable reference layouts regex written . row read either pass finds ; conflict LLM wins warning flags . Core principle: package extracts converts – invent. Every LLM-proposed variable passes hard gate ADaM specification, variable absent spec rejected logged, never shipped. Every variable ARS output traces back real annotation grounded study's ADaM spec. See "arsbridge reads annotated shell" vignette.

title: arsbridge: convert annotated TLF shells to CDISC ARS v1.0 JSON — arsbridge-package
text: LLM optional. spec_to_ars() runs one three tiers, resolved : deterministic (shell + spec – regex plus heuristics), supplement (JSON file chat assistant Copilot produces uploaded shell + spec, fed via spec_to_ars(supplement =) API call), llm (API key set). missing key never stops run. Start -API path ars_copilot_instructions(); see vignette("-api-access").

title: Convert annotated TLF shell and ADaM spec to CDISC ARS JSON — spec_to_ars
text: shell_path Path annotated TLF shells .docx. adam_spec_path Path ADaM specification. Accepts either: .xml – ADaM define.xml (preferred available) .xlsx / .xls – ADaM specification Excel (fallback used development define.xml produced) One two required. SDTM spec valid input – TLF annotations reference ADaM variables, grounding source must ADaM spec. sap_path Optional path Statistical Analysis Plan .docx. supplied, prose matched per TLF carried analysis sapDescription, becoming human-readable comment emitted {cards} block. Gracefully ignored absent unreadable. output_path Path ARS JSON. Defaults reporting_event.json tempdir(); pass explicit path write somewhere permanent. study_id Study identifier. Default "STUDY-001". study_name Human-readable study name. Defaults study_id. model LLM model. Defaults active provider's default model. api_key LLM API key. Defaults active provider's key. provider LLM provider: "anthropic", "openai", "gemini". Defaults active provider. supplement Optional path supplement .json produced chat assistant instruction file written ars_copilot_instructions(). supplied, live LLM calls made (even key set): supplement's bindings fill rows deterministic pass left unannotated (shell annotations always win; disagreements WARN findings) per-TLF fields feed enrichment path live LLM answer . Every supplement variable passes hard ADaM-spec gate. Pre-flight file ars_validate_supplement(). three tiers optional except deterministic baseline: neither supplement API key pipeline still runs end--end regex + keyword heuristics (reduced accuracy variant layouts, groupings, analysis typing; one WARN finding records mode). spec_column_aliases Optional named list extra column-name aliases ADaM spec Excel (see parse_adam_spec()); useful workbook uses non-standard non-English headers. Example: list(variable = "nom de variable", dataset = "domaine"). extract_with_llm TRUE (default), LLM re-reads section's raw shell cells primary annotation reader, separating display label variable reference variant layouts. Every proposed DATASET.VARIABLE gated ADaM spec – --spec proposals rejected logged blockers, never shipped. API key pass degrades deterministic regex result emits one warning. Set FALSE use deterministic parsing . ship_annotations FALSE (default), programmer annotation lines found outside stub cells (e.g. red Label -> DATASET.VAR paragraphs table) kept row binding validation report NEVER emitted ARS Footnote display section – rendered footnotes contain true footnotes. Set TRUE append footnotes (debug escape hatch). validate TRUE (default), cross-reference annotations ADaM spec write validation report. report_path Path validation report .xlsx. Defaults spec_validation_report.xlsx tempdir(). code_dir Directory emitted per-TLF pure-{cards} .R deliverables. NULL (default) code/ folder next output_path used. scripts human-readable deliverable engine ars_to_ard() sources build ARD. adam_dir ADaM directory baked emitted script's header (reader can edit ). Default ".". verbose Print progress messages. Default TRUE.

title: Convert annotated TLF shell and ADaM spec to CDISC ARS JSON — spec_to_ars
text: Invisibly returns named list: ars_path Path generated ARS JSON file. extraction_mode tier ran: "llm", "supplement", "deterministic". Also stored JSON _meta.extraction_mode. report_path Path validation report (validate=TRUE). code_dir Directory holding emitted per-TLF {cards} .R deliverables. code_paths Named character vector emitted .R paths (names = output ids). n_tlfs Number TLF sections processed. n_analyses Number ARS Analysis objects created. n_warnings Number spec validation warnings. reporting_event full ARS ReportingEvent nested R list – content serialised ars_path. Inspect interactively e.g. str(res$reporting_event, max.level = 2). validation Data frame per-annotation validation results (tlf_number, stub_label, annotation, variable_ref, status, message). NULL validate = FALSE. diagnostics Data frame pipeline diagnostics – every fallback, parsing miss, skipped sheet, LLM failure, unknown method, dropped -clause condition recorded run (stage, severity, tlf_number, location, problem, action). Also written "Diagnostics" sheet validation report retrievable via ars_diagnostics().

title: Developer Guide: Integrating {cards} into arsbridge for Automated ARD Generation
text: document summarizes current metadata-driven pipeline details implementation plan updating arsbridge package natively generate execute Analysis Results Data (ARD) using cards package, bypassing need third-party parsers like siera.

what: 1. How the Current Process Works (Under the Hood)
title: Developer Guide: Integrating {cards} into arsbridge for Automated ARD Generation
text: Currently, end--end workflow behaves follows:
code: [Annotated Shell (docx)] + [ADaM Spec (xlsx)]     │     ▼  (1. LLM Enrichment via `arsbridge`) [Enriched Table Specifications]     │     ▼  (2. JSON Compilation via `arsbridge::build_ars_json()`) [reporting_event.json (CDISC ARS v1.0 standard + siera-compat patches)]     │     ▼  (3. Code Compilation via `siera::readARS()`) [Generated R Scripts (containing {cards} calls and templates)]     │     ▼  (4. Execution) [ARD Dataset (Standard Tidy Format)]

previous_headings: 1. How the Current Process Works (Under the Hood)
what: Why we had to patch the JSON:
title: Developer Guide: Integrating {cards} into arsbridge for Automated ARD Generation
text: arsbridge produces strict CDISC ARS v1.0 compliant JSON. siera uses custom, non-compliant schema (e.g. flat variable mappings, hardcoded code-templates, required tables contents). Without compatibility logic (flattening variables, injecting standard cards code templates, creating dummy relationships), siera’s parser crashed.

what: 2. Proposed Native Workflow (The arsbridge Package Update)
title: Developer Guide: Integrating {cards} into arsbridge for Automated ARD Generation
text: Instead relying siera middleman, arsbridge can directly execute ARS JSON specification output cards ARD. keeps user entirely within package interface RStudio:
code: [Annotated Shell]     │     ▼  (arsbridge::spec_to_ars()) [ARS JSON Specification]     │     ▼  (arsbridge::ars_to_ard()) [Final ARD Object (Tidy Data Frame)]

what: Step 4: Bind and Return the ARD
title: Developer Guide: Integrating {cards} into arsbridge for Automated ARD Generation
text: Collect individual ARD tables merge using cards::bind_ard(). workflow enables clinical programmers perform metadata-driven table generation robust, standardized way.
code: #' Execute ARS JSON and return an ARD object #' #' @param ars_path Path to the ARS JSON file. #' @param adam_dir Directory containing the ADaM datasets (.csv or .xpt). #' @return A tidy ARD data frame. #' @export ars_to_ard <- function(ars_path, adam_dir) {   # 1. Parse JSON   spec <- jsonlite::fromJSON(ars_path, simplifyVector = FALSE)      # 2. Load ADaM datasets dynamically   # (Can support reading both .csv and .xpt using haven::read_xpt)      # 3. Loop through analyses and build ARD lists   ard_list <- list()   for (ana in spec$analyses) {     # Resolve parameters...     # Filter dataset...     # Call cards::ard_* based on methodId...     # Append to list   }      # 4. Bind results into a single tidy ARD object   final_ard <- cards::bind_ard(!!!ard_list)   return(final_ard) }

title: arsbridge 0.1.0
text: Three-tier reading engine; LLM API key now optional (R/spec_to_ars.R, R/supplement.R). spec_to_ars() longer aborts without key: resolves mode — deterministic (shell + spec ): regex + keyword heuristics, one WARN recording reduced accuracy; supplement (spec_to_ars(supplement = "supplement.json")): JSON file produced chat assistant uploaded shell + spec fills annotations regex find supplies per-TLF enrichment, API call; llm (API key set): unchanged live behaviour. New exports: ars_copilot_instructions() writes static, versioned instruction file upload Copilot/ChatGPT alongside shell spec; ars_validate_supplement() pre-flights reply. Supplement bindings fill gaps — authored shell annotations win disagreement (WARN) — every proposed variable passes hard ADaM-spec gate live LLM proposal. tier recorded _meta.extraction_mode ARS JSON spec_to_ars() result. See vignette("-api-access"). ars_copilot_instructions() copies instruction file shipped inside installed package (inst/copilot/) working directory (creating target folder needed), users never touch internal package path. -API path now cross-referenced ?arsbridge, ?spec_to_ars, every ?set_*_key / ?get_active_llm help page, README (including install-time pointer), getting-started vignette. Shell-parsing robustness cross-sponsor variation (R/parse_shell_docx.R, robustness findings F1-F4). shell reader now tolerates inline headings (Table 14.1.1: Title), two-line titles, listings population line, page-header-stored titles/populations, gridSpan/vMerge merged cells multi-row headers, Word comments, highlights, tracked changes, text boxes annotation channels. Pre-merge hardening: page-header title/population adopted header’s TLF number matches body section’s; mismatch (stale template header, header belonging another TLF) refused WARN instead silently mislabelling section. multi-row nested header <w:tblHeader/> flag inferred spanned first row (subcolumn labels survive ghost stub row produced), WARN header heuristic guess. treatment-column mapping line (Treatment columns -> ADSL.TRT01A) placed right title longer misread population; now reaches bind_annotations() column-axis grouping. paragraph population wording counts population annotation population-flag reference (...FL='Y'). pre-table footnote (Note: ...) title table kept footnote instead glued onto title. Word comment carrying annotation bound even anchored data cell rather stub cell. Fuzzy stub-label matching longer lets one/two-character label (n, %) substring-match unrelated longer phrase. Known limitations, consciously deferred: page headers read single-section documents (multi-section docx per-section headers attempted); annotation highlight-exclusion list none/black ; text-box fixture uses direct w:txbxContent shape rather Word’s mc:AlternateContent wrapper. Shell layout fidelity (ADR 0003, phases 1-5). arsbridge now carries first-class model authored table layout annotated shell way rendered output: Footnote/annotation split. Programmer annotation lines outside stub cells (coloured runs, ADaM-pattern text, Label -> DATASET.VAR arrow paragraphs table) routed programmer_annotations never shipped footnotes. spec_to_ars(ship_annotations = FALSE) default; TRUE re-attaches debugging. Convention-agnostic binding. New bind_annotations() fuzzy-matches -table Label -> annotation line back stub row (-cell detections still win), splits multi-label lines (Completed / Discontinued -> ADSL.EOSSTT (COMPLETED / DISCONTINUED)) per-row value filters, captures Treatment columns -> ADSL.TRT01A line authoritative column-axis grouping. Layout persistence + -drop. build_ars_json() walks every authored stub row order: annotated rows become analyses whose method inferred deterministically annotation form (count expression -> subject count; VAR='val' -> filtered subject count; bare variable -> categorical continuous per ADaM spec), label-rows kept layout entries, annotated row whose variable resolve reserved traceable manual_pending analysis instead dropped. ordered layout persisted per output arsbridge-private _meta.shell_layout, alongside _meta.source_datasets. Layout-driven rendering + column restriction. _meta.shell_layout present, ars_render_tlf() builds stub authored labels (joined ARD analysis_id), pins authored row order, expands categorical/continuous analyses beneath authored label, renders missing rows blank (never dropped), restricts treatment columns arm levels named shell headers – population level like “Screen Failure” TRT01A longer leaks treatment column. Outputs without layout metadata render exactly . Listings/figures. LISTING section always emits MTH_LISTING analyses regardless LLM’s analysis-type guess, ars_render_figure() now resolves default dataset shell’s Source: line (_meta.source_datasets) instead assuming ADEFF. Fixed tfrmt warning (“Unable apply frmt_combine due uniqueness column/row identifiers”) using named single-parameter formats instead one-parameter frmt_combine() generated body plans. Initial release. Classification wiring (ADR 0001): capability-gated table longer reserved wholesale. build_ars_json() now classifies statistics arsbridge can compute (deterministic keyword scan section’s title, footnotes, labels) builds partial section – descriptive rows compute, detected executable method (Clopper-Pearson CI; CMH p-value “stratified ” names strata variable) appended analysis operands. residual indicators still compute (e.g. Newcombe difference) reserved manual_pending named placeholder. residual, table longer flagged unsupported – renders computed CI / CMH cells. LLM enrichment can supersede keyword layer later. Second executable descriptor: Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel p-value (ADR 0001). New exported ard_cmh_test() wraps base R’s stats::mantelhaen.test() (cardx wrapper used) returns CMH p-value one-row ARD. MTH_CMH_TEST analysis carries stratification operand (strata analysis, resolved data), arsbridge emits arsbridge::ard_cmh_test() call computes p-value (value_source = "stats"); resolvable strata degrades manual_pending stub. executable-method registry now general (.EXEC_DESCRIPTORS: value_source plus available(res) predicate per method), replacing cardx-flag. resolve_analysis() carries new strata operand. First executable descriptor: exact (Clopper-Pearson) binomial CI (ADR 0001). cardx installed, MTH_PROPORTION_CI_EXACT method longer reserved manual cell – arsbridge emits cardx::ard_categorical_ci() call computes per-arm CIs like result (value_source = "cardx"). needs operand beyond response variable treatment grouping. Without cardx cell degrades gracefully manual_pending stub. Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel Newcombe difference stay reserve-stratification / reference-group operands carried spec. cardx soft dependency (Suggests). Manual-fill round-trip + guard (ADR 0002, phase 5). computing reserved manual_pending cell validated script, analyst writes value back ARD row (stat, result_status = "manual_filled", value_source, derivation_ref) – ARD diffable, auditable data frame. New ars_validate_manual_fills() flags manual_filled cell derivation_ref value; ars_render_all() raises blocker rendering, untraceable manual number can never ship. filled cell renders value like . See vignette("getting-started") round-trip. ADR 0002 now fully implemented (phases 1-5). Partial table rendering (ADR 0002, phase 4). output arsbridge can compute part now renders: computable cells filled reserved manual_pending cell renders loud [‡ manual] marker (never blank, never NA, never number), keyed table footnote. output computable cell stays whole-table numbered placeholder, now also names reserved cells. render manifest flags partial table partial -- manual cells reserved. Partial-results traceability (ADR 0002, phases 1-3). ars_to_ard() now stamps every row provenance columns (result_status, value_source, derivation_ref, derived_by, derived_dt). declared--unexecutable method (e.g. MTH_CMH_TEST – statistic describable ARS cards/cardx executor) longer skips coerces analysis: reserves keyed manual_pending stub ARD rows (stat = NA) table cell keeps slot tied analysis/method/output. later validated manual computation fills slot rather typing orphan value rendered output. New ars_manual_worklist() lists every pending cell analyst’s checklist. capability gate longer strips analyses gated table: ARS keeps analysis declarative MTH_UNSUPPORTED_ANALYSIS method (flagged supported = FALSE capability reason), Output -> Analysis -> Method chain intact engine reserves stub cell . renderer still emits numbered placeholder partial rendering (phase 4) lands. Additive – computed results unchanged. Architecture decision records adr/: ADR 0001 sets statistical-method extensibility boundary (bound boundary, contents – descriptor contract shared ARD shape, tiered honest degradation, deterministic emission LLM classifying); ADR 0002 proposes partial results intact traceability (reserved stub ARD rows + provenance columns cell arsbridge compute keeps keyed slot validated manual fill, never orphan value). ADR 0002 plan, yet implemented. Capability gate: tables needing inferential model-based methods (Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel, Clopper-Pearson / Newcombe intervals, p-values, odds/hazard ratios, regression, ANCOVA/MMRM, NRI imputation) detected (LLM + keyword scan), raised blockers, coerced meaningless count. carried final output numbered placeholder table numbering still matches shell exactly. placeholder now reads intentional capability gate (bug) points separate validated analysis script; render failures emit distinct placeholder clearly labelled error, gate. rationale path extending coverage recorded adr/0001-statistical-method-extensibility.md. Hybrid shell reading: deterministic four-layer regex detector LLM primary reader (extract_shell_llm()) run together take union, extract many annotation variants possible. Every LLM-proposed DATASET.VARIABLE passes hard ADaM-spec gate – --spec proposals rejected logged blockers, never shipped. API key reader degrades deterministic pass. See vignette("reading-engine"). Provider registry (R/llm_providers.R): Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, OpenAI-compatible providers GLM defined one place. Adding provider single entry. New generic set_llm_key() setter; select active provider ARS_LLM_PROVIDER. spec_to_ars(): parse annotated TLF shell .docx plus ADaM specification (define.xml Excel) CDISC Analysis Results Standard (ARS) v1.0 JSON. ars_to_ard(): execute ARS JSON natively tidy Analysis Results Data (ARD) object via cards, applying analysisSets dataSubsets filters .xpt / .csv datasets. ars_render_tlf(), ars_render_all(), ars_to_tfrmt(): render ARS outputs publication-ready GT Word tables via tfrmt. Multi-provider LLM enrichment (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini) keyword-heuristic fallback pipeline runs without API key. ars_diagnostics() / ars_blockers(): plain-English diagnostics point user input document fix.
